samples.gvd:
module dual_port_ram #(
    parameter DEPTH = 16,
    parameter WIDTH = 8,
    parameter ADDR_WIDTH = $clog2(DEPTH)
) (
    input wclk,
    input wenc,
    input [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] waddr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata,
    input rclk,
    input renc,
    input [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] raddr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata
);

    reg [WIDTH-1:0] mem [DEPTH-1:0];

    always @(posedge wclk) begin
        if (wenc) begin
            mem[waddr] <= wdata;
        end
    end

    always @(posedge rclk) begin
        if (renc) begin
            rdata <= mem[raddr];
        end
    end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller. Write clock. Read clock. Active low reset. Write increment. Read increment. Write data. Write full. Read empty. Read data. Data width of FIFO. Depth of FIFO.

Implement an asynchronous FIFO with the specified depth and width.

The FIFO should be implemented using dual-port RAM.

The FIFO should be implemented using two separate controllers for writing and reading.

The FIFO should be implemented using two separate pointers for writing and reading.

The two pointers should be synchronized using two-stage synchronizers.

The synchronizers should be implemented using two flip-flops.

The FIFO should be reset using the reset signal.

The FIFO should be implemented using combinational logic.
